title: steiroctis capitata
intro: Steiroctis capitata, also known as the 'False Buttonbush', is a flowering plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and rounded clusters. Steiroctis capitata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Steiroctis capitata is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
